h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to practice identifying the beat unit and counting tiles.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [writes 3/8 below the bar, then puts the quarter-note card back over the lower box] Three tiles stop at the bar line, so the top box stays 3. With this card back, the lower box changes to 4.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully writes the time signature 3/8 and performs the card swap to demonstrate understanding of the beat unit.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical covering and uncovering mechanism to help the learner separate the functions of the top and bottom numbers.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The physical covering and uncovering mechanism isolates the two numbers, preventing the learner from conflating the count with the beat unit.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [writes 3/8 below the bar, then puts the quarter-note card back over the lower box] Three tiles stop at the bar line, so the top box stays 3. With this card back, the lower box changes to 4.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully writes the time signature 3/8 and performs the card swap to demonstrate understanding of the beat unit.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical covering and uncovering mechanism to help the learner separate the functions of the top and bottom numbers.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical covering and uncovering mechanism to help the learner separate the functions of the top and bottom numbers.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The physical covering and uncovering mechanism isolates the two numbers, preventing the learner from conflating the count with the beat unit.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to apply the 'object first, direction second' rule to place the remaining cards.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Object first: track on cart, if this is the upward push. Then the arrow direction is up. If this slot is the down arrow, it is Earth on cart instead.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies the 'object first' rule to identify the track and Earth as the acting objects for the vertical forces.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown that triggers a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism was triggered as the learner followed the instructions successfully.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to respond to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Object first: track on cart, if this is the upward push. Then the arrow direction is up. If this slot is the down arrow, it is Earth on cart instead.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently applies the 'object first' rule to identify the track and Earth as the acting objects for the vertical forces.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to respond to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown that triggers a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown that triggers a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism was triggered as the learner followed the instructions successfully.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The form-label idea helps, but I still keep wanting `rainfall_mm` to stand for the whole rainfall column.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with separating the column header's role from the individual row values.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The row sentence is: Cedar Falls recorded 42 millimeters of rainfall in May. The header `rainfall_mm` still does not claim every row is 42, and it still does not make 42 an average.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the sentence-generation task and applies the constraints to show what the header does not claim.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The row sentence is: Cedar Falls recorded 42 millimeters of rainfall in May. The header `rainfall_mm` still does not claim every row is 42, and it still does not make 42 an average.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully performs the sentence-generation task and applies the constraints to show what the header does not claim.
